How to use this form
For each item: review the photo (circles/markings show the exact work areas) and the description. Then choose Yes or No. Scroll to the bottom when done to copy your selections and send them back.
01

Palm Tree by the Car

📍 Front driveway
HIGH PRIORITY
SITUATION

A fallen/damaged palm tree near the vehicle in the front driveway. Roots are fully exposed and the tree has toppled over.

WORK SCOPE
  • Full removal of downed palm tree
  • Root base removal
  • Complete debris cleanup
⚠ If skipped: Immediate safety hazard — obstructs driveway and could damage vehicle
✅ Already confirmed — this will be included
Include this work?
02

Backyard Oak Tree

📍 Rear yard, near pool/patio
MEDIUM PRIORITY
SITUATION

Large oak in the backyard. Lower hanging limbs pull the tree's weight down (circled bottom area). Dense interior sucker growth (circled middle) crowds the canopy.

WORK SCOPE
  • Remove lower hanging limbs to encourage upright growth
  • Remove interior sucker growth to open canopy for wind mitigation
⚠ If skipped: Lower limbs could contact patio structure; dense canopy increases storm load
Include this work?
03

Norfolk Pine Trees + Stump Grinding

📍 Backyard, along fence
HIGH PRIORITY
SITUATION

Two Norfolk Pines showing heavy browning (mostly dead). Located close to the fence line. Full removal and stump grinding is a separate line item on the quote.

WORK SCOPE
  • Complete removal of both Norfolk Pine trees
  • Stump grinding (separate quote line item)
  • Full debris cleanup
⚠ If skipped: Dead/dying trees near fence — risk of fall and fence/structure damage
Include this work?
04

Small Dead Tree (Near Norfolk Pine)

📍 Backyard, in front of Norfolk Pines
MEDIUM PRIORITY
SITUATION

A small fully dead tree (no foliage) directly in front of the Norfolk Pines. Contractor has circled the entire tree for removal.

WORK SCOPE
  • Full removal of dead tree
  • Debris cleanup included
⚠ If skipped: Dead tree is a fall hazard — can topple without warning
Include this work?
05

Dead Yucca Palm (Back Left Corner)

📍 Back left corner of fence
MEDIUM PRIORITY
SITUATION

A large Yucca Palm in the back left corner where two fence sections meet. It has grown into and over the fence panels (circled).

WORK SCOPE
  • Complete removal of Yucca Palm
  • Root and base removal
  • Full debris cleanup
⚠ If skipped: Actively growing into fence — causing structural damage to fence panels
Include this work?
06

Front Right Oak Tree

📍 Front right side of house
HIGH PRIORITY
SITUATION

Large oak on the front right. Two lower branches are circled at the bottom hanging toward the house. The word "Sucker Growth" is written on the canopy to show interior branches to clean out.

WORK SCOPE
  • Remove two lower hanging branches (hanging toward house)
  • Remove downward-growing branches (weight reduction, reduces property damage risk)
  • Clean out sucker growth and dead limbs from center canopy
⚠ If skipped: Branches hanging toward roof — potential for damage during storms
Include this work?
07

Camphor Tree (Front Yard)

📍 Front yard
MEDIUM PRIORITY
SITUATION

Large Camphor tree in the front yard. Marked branches to be removed, plus a large circled area showing sucker growth and dead limbs throughout the canopy. Additional branches will be identified once crew is in the tree.

WORK SCOPE
  • Remove all marked branches
  • Remove sucker growth and dead limbs (circled area)
  • Additional cleanup once crew is elevated
⚠ If skipped: Dead limbs and dense canopy increase risk of branch failure
Include this work?
08

Cedar Tree (Right Side of House)

📍 Right side yard
LOWER PRIORITY
SITUATION

Tall cedar along the right side yard walkway. The circled area shows dead lower limbs that need removal.

WORK SCOPE
  • Remove dead limbs from bottom section of cedar
  • Improves clearance along the walkway
⚠ If skipped: Dead lower limbs could fall onto walkway or siding
Include this work?

Smaller Oak Tree (Front Left Corner)

📍 Front left, behind fence corner
MEDIUM PRIORITY
SITUATION

A smaller oak tucked in the front left corner. Contractor circled lower branches growing toward the fence and sucker growth at the base that's creeping back into the fence.

WORK SCOPE
  • Remove lower branches growing down toward fence (upper circle)
  • Remove sucker growth at base to prevent regrowth into fence (lower circle)
⚠ If skipped: Branches and sucker growth actively damaging fence panels

Neighbor's Oak — Driveway Overhang

📍 Front driveway edge
LOWER PRIORITY
SITUATION

Limbs from the neighbor's oak tree hang over the driveway (circled). Low enough to affect vehicle clearance and drop debris on vehicles.

WORK SCOPE
  • Trim lower hanging limbs overhanging driveway
  • Improves vehicle clearance, reduces debris drop
⚠ If skipped: Debris drop on vehicles; clearance concern for taller vehicles
Include this work?

Roadside Palm Debris Hauling

📍 Roadside / front of property
LOWER PRIORITY
SITUATION

A pile of palm tree debris (fronds, organic material) left at the roadside by a third party. Homeowner asked if crew could haul this away as part of the job.

WORK SCOPE
  • Load and haul away roadside palm debris pile
  • No cutting required — hauling only
⚠ If skipped: Eyesore; potential code/HOA violation for roadside debris
